# Ant Code v1.2 TUI Acceptance And Closure

Date: 2026-04-29

## Scope

This note records the current v1.2 TUI polish acceptance state for the internal
lab build. It focuses on the clean-room TUI stages from
`docs/specs/tui-v1.2-interaction-polish-outline.md`.

## Accepted

Stages 1 through 6 are accepted for the current internal build:

- Stage 1 renderer/theme: semantic theme tokens, built-in sky-blue/default/no
  color themes, and bounded frame tests exist.
- Stage 2 composer/input: cursor-aware editing, wide-character handling,
  readline-style deletion/movement, raw Backspace/Delete fallbacks, and
  bounded prompt tests exist.
- Stage 3 popovers/interrupts: popover priority, two-press busy `Esc`, direct
  `Ctrl+G`, two-press `Ctrl+C`, and visible interrupt feedback exist.
- Stage 4 transcript/streaming: the user accepted long-answer streaming,
  mouse-wheel review, right-side panel stability, and `Ctrl+O`
  compact/detail/full expansion in the real TUI.
- Stage 5 tool/permission UX: the user accepted a live create/write/read file
  workflow where the write permission confirmation box appeared and was
  operable.
- Stage 6 command product surfaces: the user accepted `/help`, `/status`,
  `/model`, `/context`, `/usage`, `/cost`, `/permissions`, `/gateway`, and
  `/keybindings` as readable and usable panels.

## Stage 7 Closure Decision

Stage 7 is accepted for the current controlled internal build. The release
decision combines direct live validation from the lab operator with automated
coverage for queue, file mention, resume, shortcut, guide, and compact edge
cases that are hard to exhaustively reproduce in a physical terminal.

- [x] `/sessions` title-first picker remains readable and navigable.
- [x] `/resume latest` restores retained transcript messages when available.
- [x] `/resume <unique-visible-id-prefix>` resolves the intended session.
- [x] Busy normal prompts enter `/queue`; delete/promote/run helpers and busy
  routing are covered by `tests/unit/tui-workflows.test.js`.
- [x] `@` file mentions show fuzzy/recent candidates and insert cleanly; fuzzy,
  recent, workspace-boundary, and insertion behavior are covered by
  `tests/unit/tui-palettes.test.js`.
- [x] `/guide <message>` during a long task waits for the response boundary
  when needed, interrupts before tools or the next model request, and runs the
  guide next.
- [x] `/guide 停止` cancels without creating a guided continuation prompt.
- [x] `/compact` is callable in the live TUI and was confirmed usable by the
  lab operator. Current implementation prefers a separate model summarization
  request through the configured lab gateway, keeps recent messages exactly,
  reports `模型摘要` when that request succeeds, and falls back to local
  deterministic compaction with a visible reason when the gateway is
  unavailable or summarization fails.
- [x] Shortcut release smoke is accepted for the current build for `Ctrl+O`,
  `Tab`, `Shift+Tab`,
  `Ctrl+T`, `Ctrl+N/P`, `Ctrl+F/B`, `Ctrl+R/E` when patch data exists,
  `PageUp/PageDown`, busy `Esc` twice, `Ctrl+G`, and two-press `Ctrl+C`;
  advertised shortcut copy and raw terminal fallbacks are covered by unit tests,
  with patch-specific `Ctrl+R/E` remaining naturally conditional on patch data.

## Stage 8 Release Posture

Stage 8 is partial but non-blocking for the current internal build:

- MCP stdio runtime exists and is tested.
- `/mcp` can list configured servers, list tools, and call configured tools
  through the permission boundary.
- Readonly subagent execution exists through `/agents run readonly-researcher`.
  It is a local slash-command runner backed by built-in read-only tools, not an
  MCP-dependent workflow.
- Write-capable subagents intentionally return an unsupported profile error.
- `/skills`, richer task/subagent panels, background work, branch/rewind/stash,
  and custom extension panels remain deferred. They are surfaced as disabled or
  local-replacement states rather than broken commands.

Release decision: Stage 8 should be described as "partial, non-blocking" unless
the lab specifically requires richer extension panels for the handoff.

## Stage 9 Release Hardening Checklist

- [x] `npm run check` passes after the final documentation/code refresh.
  Latest run on 2026-04-29 passed with 241 tests.
- [x] `npm run verify:release` passes before packaging or handoff freeze.
  Latest run on 2026-04-29 passed.
- [x] Generated audit/release artifacts are refreshed if release verification
  regenerates them. Latest `verify:release` regenerated endpoint, SBOM,
  license, provenance, RC, and audit report artifacts.
- [x] Final Stage 7 live smoke and release decision are recorded in this file
  and the handoff.
- [x] Live gateway compatibility evidence is attached, or gateway live testing
  is explicitly deferred with a reason. Current deferral: local environment has
  no `LAB_MODEL_GATEWAY_URL` / `LAB_MODEL_GATEWAY_HEALTH_URL`; mock gateway
  compatibility passed, and lab-operator live evidence is still required before
  broad model-enabled rollout.
- [x] Candidate commit hash or package SHA256 is recorded outside the package
  before distribution.

## Current Risk Notes

- The TUI relies on real-terminal behavior for mouse wheel, raw key sequences,
  resize, and alternate-screen handling. The current high-risk flows have live
  acceptance and unit coverage, but future terminals should still be smoke
  tested before broad rollout.
- `/compact` model summarization is implemented and accepted for the controlled
  build. Automated tests cover model-request shape, fallback behavior, and
  continued use of the compacted summary; live lab-gateway compatibility remains
  part of the lab operator rollout gate.
- Stage 8 deferred items are intentionally visible as disabled/local-only
  replacement states to avoid implying cloud marketplace or remote execution
  support.
- Do not write gateway API keys or provider credentials into repository files.
